title: Arabic I
catalogDescription: A beginning language course focusing on the study of Modern Standard Arabic. It develops the ability to read, speak, listen and write in Arabic through the consideration of cultural themes, language functions, and authentic situations. Stresses communication using both formal and informal Arabic and exposes the students to the diversity and social issues of the Arab-speaking world.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 . No previous background in the study of this language.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Arabic II
catalogDescription: The second level of the first year of Modern Standard Arabic language used throughout the Arab World. Develops the four communicative language skills: listening, speaking, reading, and writing. It stresses communication skills and emphasizes the links between language, culture and history.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 and ARAB 121 with a grade of 2.0 or higher; or equivalent with instructor consent. The equivalent of ARAB 121 is one year in high school with a grade of B or higher.

title: Arabic III
catalogDescription: The third level of the first year of the Modern Standard Arabic language used throughout the Arab World. Develops the four communicative language skills: listening, speaking, reading, and writing at the appropriate level. Stresses communication skills and emphasizes understanding the connections between the language with the culture and diversity of the Arab-speaking World.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 and ARAB 122 with a grade of 2.0 or higher; or equivalent with instructor consent. The equivalent of ARAB 122 is two years in high school with a grade of B or higher.
